ontology.foodex2_description: Chicken meat, cooked — used as closest FoodEx2 anchor for marinated grilled chicken tikka kebab.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of Chicken Tikka Kebab in Singapore is approximately 150g, equivalent to 3–4 skewers of marinated grilled chicken pieces.

nutrient_source.title: Chicken Tikka Kebab — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Chicken Tikka Kebab (cr_id G00010786). All per_100g values are taken from the HPB record with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated at 5.5g/100g based on ICMR-NIN reference for similar marinated grilled chicken preparations, as HPB record does not separately report saturated fat.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — analogous estimate based on marinated grilled chicken with yogurt and spice marinade
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Chicken Tikka Kebab in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at approximately 30 (low) based on the analogous food category of marinated grilled chicken with yogurt-based marinade. Yogurt has a GI of approximately 14–36 (University of Sydney GI Database); chicken meat itself has negligible carbohydrate and does not raise blood glucose. The spice marinade contributes minimal carbohydrate. GL per 150g serving = (30 × 5.24 × 1.5) / 100 = 2.36, rounded to 2.4.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 1080mg/100g — far exceeds the 600mg/100g upper threshold for hypertension-safe foods.
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit Chicken Tikka Kebab due to its very high sodium content of 1080mg/100g; if consumed, portion should not exceed 80–100g and must be counted against the daily sodium budget of 2000mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low carbohydrate (5.24g/100g) and low sugar (2.85g/100g) with high protein supporting glycaemic stability; estimated GI approximately 30 (low).
type2_diabetes.guidance: Chicken Tikka Kebab is suitable for Type 2 Diabetes management due to its low carbohydrate content and low estimated GI; however, the high fat content (26.4g/100g) warrants portion control to approximately 100–150g, and it should not be served with high-GI accompaniments such as naan or white rice.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Sodium 1080mg/100g and saturated fat approximately 5.5g/100g both elevate cardiovascular risk; total fat 26.4g/100g is also elevated.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should limit Chicken Tikka Kebab to occasional consumption in small portions (≤100g) due to its high sodium and saturated fat content, which contribute to blood pressure elevation and atherogenic risk; preparation with reduced salt and leaner chicken cuts is recommended.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Saturated fat approximately 5.5g/100g exceeds the 5g/100g RA threshold; sodium 1080mg/100g worsens tissue swelling and inflammation.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid regular consumption of Chicken Tikka Kebab due to its high sodium and borderline-elevated saturated fat content, which may promote pro-inflammatory cytokine activity; turmeric and ginger in the marinade provide modest anti-inflammatory benefit but are insufficient to offset the sodium and fat burden.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Chicken Tikka Kebab is a popular Indian-origin grilled chicken dish widely consumed as street food in Singapore and across South and Southeast Asia. The dish consists of boneless chicken pieces — typically breast or thigh — marinated in a spiced yogurt mixture containing turmeric, cumin, coriander, red chilli powder, ginger, garlic, garam masala, and salt, then skewered and cooked over a charcoal grill, tandoor oven, or open flame to achieve a characteristic smoky char. The result is tender, juicy chicken with a slightly crisp exterior, bold spiced aroma, and a mildly tangy flavour from the yogurt marinade. Per 100g, the dish provides 302 kcal, 10.7g protein, 26.4g fat, 5.24g carbohydrate, and 1080mg sodium. The high fat content reflects the oil and yogurt used in the marinade and the cooking method. Sodium is very high at 1080mg/100g, making this dish unsuitable for hypertension and cardiovascular disease patients without significant modification. The low carbohydrate content and estimated low glycaemic index of approximately 30 make it suitable for Type 2 Diabetes management when portion-controlled and served without high-GI accompaniments. Turmeric and ginger in the marinade contribute anti-inflammatory phytonutrients. The dish is halal-certified in most Singapore hawker and restaurant contexts and is gluten-free by default. It is commonly served at lunch and dinner as a main dish or starter, often accompanied by mint chutney, sliced onions, and lemon wedges.
common_user_queries: How many calories in chicken tikka kebab?
Is chicken tikka kebab suitable for diabetics?
Can I eat chicken tikka kebab if I have high blood pressure?
What is the difference between chicken tikka and chicken tikka kebab?
How much protein is in chicken tikka kebab?
Is chicken tikka kebab gluten free?

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010786) with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated at 5.5g/100g from ICMR-NIN reference for analogous marinated grilled chicken preparation. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database framework. All other values are exact from the HPB record.
notes: NEGATIVE SENTINEL VALUES (-1) DOCUMENTATION: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, and trans_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions for Chicken Tikka Kebab are not separately reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry G00010786, nor in the ICMR-NIN Indian Food Composition Tables for this specific preparation as of 2026. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, and vitamin_e_mg set to -1: micronutrient vitamin data for Chicken Tikka Kebab are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry G00010786 or in the ICMR-NIN Indian Food Composition Tables for this specific preparation as of 2026. phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, and selenium_mcg set to -1: these mineral values are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry G00010786 or in the ICMR-NIN Indian Food Composition Tables for this specific preparation as of 2026. water_g set to -1: moisture content for Chicken Tikka Kebab is not separately reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry G00010786 as of 2026. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 as reported by HPB; this is plausible for a meat-dominant dish with minimal vegetable content. saturated_fat_g of 5.5g/100g is estimated from ICMR-NIN reference data for marinated grilled chicken tikka preparations, as the HPB record does not separately itemise saturated fat. GI of 30 is an analogous estimate based on the University of Sydney GI Database framework for yogurt-marinated grilled chicken; no direct GI measurement for Chicken Tikka Kebab was found in the database as of 2026. Ontology: FoodOn FOODON_03302757 (chicken kebab food product) used as closest_parent match; FoodEx2 A0FJP (cooked chicken meat) used as closest_parent defensible anchor. Halal status reflects standard Singapore hawker/restaurant preparation; individual vendor verification recommended.
